xposts.title: Agency as control over future possibilities
xposts.excerpt: Our past and our future fall apart into possibilities, beyond our ability to model and control, including the past and future of our own identity. Agency is the ability to take control of the future

xposts.title: Transcendence via coherent participation in higher agency
xposts.excerpt: We achieve transcendence by coherently participating in the next layer of agency. Instead of the dream of the individual, we dream the dream of the culture, the civilization, of life and conscious organization.

xposts.title: Mind as resonator creating organization for resonance
xposts.excerpt: It's interesting to look at the mind as a resonator, a system that creates organization and structure to maintain near perfect resonance with the patterns created by the sensory interface, and conscious attention as the part that weaves Turing machines

xposts.title: Mental states exist only as representations in models
xposts.excerpt: Computers will never understand anything, feel anything, want anything, for the same reasons that brains cannot understand, feel and desire. Mental states only exist as representations, i.e. in the functional frames of models, not in the frames of physical implementations.

xposts.title: Free will requires self-model as agent
xposts.excerpt: The difference between a system with free will and one without it is that the former performs its control based on a model of itself as an agent

xposts.title: Civilization-level sentience via religion and collective minds
xposts.excerpt: If we understand how religion projects a civilization into the future by merging individual minds into a collective, transcendental civilization-level sentience, we also see the need for religious institutions.

xposts.title: Podcast on AGI alignment, consciousness and digital physics
xposts.excerpt: New podcast! – with Future of Life Institute's @AnthonyNAguirre, Lucas Perry and me, about truth, physics, computability, consciousness, and AGI alignment

xposts.title: Rejecting embodied agent view for virtual machine principles
xposts.excerpt: Many philosophers have this concept of intelligence as a kind of embodied agent in a physical world, you could call it the "interactive meat" mindset, instead of the organizational principles of a virtual machine that can create mathematical and physical worlds as it sees fit

terms.title: agency
terms.definition: The ability to take control of the future by coherently participating in higher layers of organization, from individual to civilization-scale. It requires a model of oneself as an agent performing control.

terms.title: sentience
terms.definition: Transcendental collective organization emerging when individual minds merge into civilization-level structures, as projected by religion or shared dreams of the future.
